markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Persist folder hierarchy, labels, colors, sort modes, and project membership in PostgreSQL. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: FolderTreeAndColorLabels leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: FolderTreeAndColorLabels is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Persistent Loom folder hierarchy + color labels + sort modes + project membership in PostgreSQL. Organizational overlay over LoomBlocks; never a second source of block truth. No parallel store.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0294_loom_folders.sql (NEW) - loom_folders (self-referential parent_folder_id, color, sort_mode CHECK vocab, manual sort_order, soft project_ref; sibling-name uniqueness; not-self-parent CHECK) + loom_folder_members (folder<->block overlay, manual sort_order)

src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - impls + map_loom_folder; list tree parent-before-child (recursive CTE); update move guarded by descendant-cycle + self-parent checks; list_loom_folder_blocks ORDER BY chosen by sort_mode (fixed literals)

tests: folder_tree_persists_hierarchy_color_and_sort - nested tree, recolor+rename, parent-before-child listing, sort_mode persisted
folder_membership_and_manual_sort - manual order C,A,B; idempotent re-add (reorder, no dup); remove
folder_name_sort_mode_orders_by_title - name_asc orders Ant before Zebra
folder_move_into_own_subtree_is_rejected - descendant-cycle move + self-parent both rejected
deleting_folder_cascades_subtree_but_not_blocks - delete root cascades child + membership; the LoomBlock survives

evidence.summary: Crash recovery to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after independent adversarial review PASS and fresh current-tree real-PostgreSQL proof for Loom folder hierarchy/color labels. This is Kernel Builder handoff readiness only; validator review remains pending.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

findings: MOVE CYCLE (handled+tested): re-parenting walks up from the proposed parent via a recursive CTE; if it reaches the folder being moved, the move is rejected. Self-parent rejected separately. (DB also has a not-self-parent CHECK.)
OVERLAY NOT OWNERSHIP (handled+tested): deleting a folder cascades the subtree + membership rows but never the LoomBlocks; a block can be in multiple folders.
SET/CLEAR SEMANTICS (handled): LoomFolderUpdate uses Option<Option<T>> + CASE WHEN $flag so a field can be left unchanged, set, or explicitly cleared (color, sort_order, parent, project_ref).
ORDER-BY INJECTION (clear): list_loom_folder_blocks picks a fixed &str ORDER BY by sort_mode enum; folder name/color/project_ref are bound params under CHECK constraints.
SIBLING-NAME COLLISION (handled by schema): uq_loom_folders_sibling_name (workspace, parent, name) prevents ambiguous duplicate paths; surfaced as a Conflict/Database error.
FAIL-CLOSED (tested): create under a missing parent, and adding a missing block, both error; project_ref is a documented soft ref (no hard FK) per MT-152 typed-reference discipline.
DOS (mitigated): list_loom_folder_blocks clamps limit 1..500; the tree CTE is bounded by the workspace folder count.
fixes: No code change required; findings handled-by-design and tested.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-181 requires PostgreSQL-persistent Loom folder hierarchy with color labels, sort modes, and project membership as an organizational overlay over LoomBlocks (never a second source of block truth). Commit 3a872e4e delivers exactly this with real schema, real PostgresDatabase CRUD, cycle/overlay/fail-closed guards, REST routes, and 6 real-Postgres integration tests. Code is real, spec-aligned, and runtime-proven against managed PostgreSQL.
findings: migrations/0294_loom_folders.sql:16-67 — real schema: loom_folders (self-referential parent_folder_id ON DELETE CASCADE, color, sort_mode CHECK vocab name_asc/name_desc/created_desc/updated_desc/manual, manual sort_order, soft project_ref, not-self-parent CHECK, sibling-name UNIQUE) + loom_folder_members overlay (FK to loom_blocks ON DELETE CASCADE). Matches MT scope exactly.

storage/postgres.rs (commit 3a872e4e) — real PostgresDatabase impls: list_loom_folders uses recursive CTE for parent-before-child ordering; update_loom_folder has descendant-cycle guard (recursive walk-up) + self-parent rejection; list_loom_folder_blocks picks ORDER BY from sort_mode via fixed &str literals (no injection); add_block_to_loom_folder is idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) and fail-closed (verifies folder+block exist); delete cascades subtree+membership but never blocks.

storage/loom.rs (commit 3a872e4e) — LoomFolder/NewLoomFolder/LoomFolderUpdate (Option<Option<T>> set/clear semantics) + LoomFolderSortMode enum with as_str/FromStr; enum vocab matches the SQL CHECK constraint exactly.
api/loom.rs (commit 3a872e4e) — /loom/folders CRUD + /loom/folders/:id/blocks(/:block_id) routes wired to the real storage methods.

tests/loom_folder_tests.rs:81-253 — 6 integration tests against real PostgreSQL: hierarchy/color/sort persistence, manual+name sort ordering, move-into-own-subtree rejection, delete-cascades-subtree-but-not-blocks, fail-closed missing parent/block. tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:21,40,108-155 connects via POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL into an isolated schema with real migrations and explicitly NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback — satisfies sub_rule_2.

storage/mod.rs trait default methods return StorageError::NotImplemented for non-Postgres implementors only; this is the codebase's standard non-authority default pattern, NOT a reachable deferred-live escape — the PostgreSQL authority path (the path the MT requires and the tests exercise) is fully implemented.

Spec §7.1.1.9 (07-user-experience-and-development.md:280) normatively lists 'folder linking with folder color labels' and mandates no parallel store / no markdown-vault authority; §7.1.4.3 frames Loom surfaces as DisplayContent projections over one LoomBlock dataset. The overlay-not-ownership implementation matches.

ASIDE (non-blocking paperwork): the cited green-test log path ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-009-recovery/pg-external-loom-wiki-20260614-205058/green-mt181-loom-folder-tests.log does not exist on disk; evidence-reference drift only, code+tests are real and runnable.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
